body:
### Problem

Many LSP features are supported via the `decoration_provider`. Their commonality lies in allowing the LSP server to control `extmark`s within the buffer, and these `extmark`s are synchronized with the document version through continuous LSP requests. These features include:
- Semantic tokens
- Inlay hint
- Code lens
- Document color
- Document link

Previously, we introduced `Capability`, which unifies LSP data lifecycle management and standardizes the `enable` syntax(#28603). However, some of these modules have not yet fully utilized it, including:
- **Inlay hint:** Because it was implemented earlier, it handles its data lifecycle and `enable` syntax control completely independently, differing from the other modules.
- **Document link:** Currently, it only supports manual triggering, rather than utilizing `OSC 8` to make the terminal aware of the links' existence. #37691

It is worth further refactoring `inlay hint` and `document link` to use `Capability`. This will improve maintainability, unify their behavioral logic, and make bug fixing easier.

---
Furthermore, among the features mentioned above, semantic tokens is the only ones that differ in request patterns. All the others synchronize data via `textDocument/{method}` and fetch more detailed information via `{method}/resolve`. 

The differences in their implementations largely come down to how the `extmark` is displayed. It might be worthwhile to introduce an API specifically for setting extmark in a document-synchronized manner. This would not only reduce boilerplate code but also facilitate extensibility benefits to resolve #32035.

---
Note that in addition to the methods above, there are a few similar variants:
- Where the target being controlled is fold information rather than `extmark`s:
  - Folding range
- Where synchronization is required not just with the document version, but also with the cursor/mouse position:
  - Document highlight

### Expected behavior

TBD

labels.description: changes that are not features or bugfixes
labels.name: refactor

title: Improve the LSP method based on `decoration_provider`, consider exposing APIs

type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality
type.name: Enhancement
